    Tsung-Han Yang

    Postdoctoral Researcher @ ORNL
    PhD in Physics @ Brown University

    • Knoxville, TN
    • Email
    • GitHub
    • LinkedIn

    Bridging Physics & Computation

    Postdoctoral Research Fellow @ Oak Ridge National Laboratory

    I stand at the intersection of experimental physics and computational engineering. By integrating multi-modal scattering (Neutron/X-ray) with advanced statistical analytics, I decode the hidden behaviors of complex systems to transform noisy experimental data into predictive physical models.

    Condensed Matter Physics

    Focusing on topological Weyl magnets, correlated disorder, and phonon dynamics in quantum systems.

    Neutron Scattering Magnetism Phase Transitions

    Explore Research Projects →

    Scientific Software

    Building robust tools for data inversion (RMC), molecular dynamics, and algorithmic modeling.

    C++17 Python (HPC) Machine Learning

    View Packages & Tools →

    Recent Highlights

    • [Publication] Intrinsic Topological Weyl Phase Transition… — Nat. Commun. (2026)
    • [Software] Released v2.1 of rmc-phonon-dynamics with improved MPI support.
    • [Note] New learning notes on Bayesian Optimization for Physics.
